name: Inreach NC
quote: 157 Living support was provided to 84 individuals in our 24/7 staffed group homes. 235 individuals were provided with job support, helping them secure employment, complete job training, and thrive as valued members of their company’s workforce. Provided assistance to over 400 individuals, covering expenses such as rent, utilities, therapy sessions, car repairs, and more through our First In Families project.
description: InReach is a recognized leader in providing housing, employment, and community support for individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities in Charlotte/Mecklenburg and nearby counties.
address: 4014 Monroe Rd Building 4 Suite 170, Charlotte, NC 28205

name: Down Syndrome Association of Greater Charlotte
quote: Don’t look away because you’re uncomfortable. Comfort starts here…
description: The Down Syndrome Association (DSA) of Greater Charlotte is an approved 501(c)3 non-profit, family support organization. The goal of the DSA of Greater Charlotte and its families is to enable individuals with Down syndrome (Ds) to reach their full potential and become respected members of their community.
address: 3900 Park Road, Suite C Charlotte, NC 28209

name: Nevins Inc.
quote: Providing exceptional person-centered care to adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities since 1959.
description: Nevins supports individuals in Mecklenburg county with developmental disabilities such as Intellectual Development Disorder and illness, epilepsy, down syndrome, cerebral palsy, traumatic brain injury, autism and learning disabilities.

name: LIFESPAN Services
quote: Our mission, and our passion, is to illuminate the abilities of children and adults with disabilities by providing education, employment, and enrichment opportunities to live, work, and play in their communities throughout their lifetimes.
description: LIFESPAN Services help to make the abilities of children and adults shine by providing education, employment, and enrichment opportunities to live, work and play in their communities throughout their life. They spend each day supporting people of all ages and abilities, guiding them toward moments of everyday joy and the simple experiences that make life meaningful.
address: 1511 Shopton Road, Suite A Charlotte, NC 28217

name: Healthy Charlotte Alliance
quote: Our Mission is to improve the health and quality of life for all people of Mecklenburg County.
description: Healthy Charlotte Alliance was founded by physicians with a desire to support a healthier Charlotte. They achieve this mission through health education, engaging volunteers, and financial investments in proven-effective health programs.
address: 338 S Sharon Amity Road, #504 Charlotte, NC 28211

name: RAIN
quote: RAIN empowers people living with HIV and those at risk to be healthy and stigma free.
description: RAIN has supported thousands in the Charlotte community and is known for being reliable, caring, and committed. They work with clients to get medical care and medications they need, offer emergency help with rent, utilities and food, and support underprivileged people with other challenges that affect their health and stability.

name: Hospitality House of Charlotte
quote: We have utilized the services of Hospitality House several times during our journey. We appreciate the services of the many volunteer groups who provide meals.
description: Hospitality House of Charlotte provides affordable housing and a supportive community for patients and families who must travel to Charlotte for critical medical care. It also offers a safe, comfortable place to stay so families can focus on healing rather than housing.

name: NAMI Family & Friends
quote: I have a better understanding of how mental illness impacts the person affected…it was nice to hear the stories of the instructors and their paths. The handbook (eBook) given has a lot of helpful information which I did not know about.
description: NAMI Family & Friends is a free 90-minute or four-hour seminar that informs people who have loved ones with a mental health condition how to best support them. It’s also an opportunity to meet other people in similar situations and gain community support.

name: Freedom School Partners
quote: Freedom School Partners is a Charlotte area non-profit with a mission to inspire our scholars to love reading and learning, believe in themselves, and create extraordinary futures. We advocate relentlessly for our scholars by focusing on educational equity, as well as the root causes of academic achievement gaps.
description: Freedom School Partners is a Charlotte area non-profit with a mission to inspire our scholars to love reading and learning, believe in themselves, and create extraordinary futures. We advocate relentlessly for our scholars by focusing on educational equity, as well as the root causes of academic achievement gaps.

name: Learning Help Centers of Charlotte
quote: Our goal is to provide scholastic, spiritual and social support to equip and empower our underserved neighbors.
description: LHCC partners with dedicated volunteers and community organizations to support the entire family, offering weekly tutoring for students, skill‑building programs for parents, summer camps that help children learn and grow, and family events that promote health, connection, and overall well‑being.

name: Augustine Literacy Project
quote: My ALP Tutor has proved to be the BEST tutor I have ever experienced. I have taught 20 years and I would take her and this program over any other tutoring program I have worked with in my years of teaching. I couldn’t be more thankful for this program and what you do to help students.
description: The Augustine Literacy Project of Charlotte provides free, long‑term, one‑on‑one literacy tutoring to young students in under‑resourced communities, using highly trained volunteers and research‑based instruction. It focuses on helping K–2 readers in Charlotte‑Mecklenburg Title I schools build strong reading, writing, and spelling skills. The organization transforms outcomes for children while empowering community members to make a direct, lasting impact.

name: Urban League
quote: ULCC's mission is to advocate for and equip African Americans and ALL underserved communities with the tools to achieve social and economic equality.
description: The Urban League of Central Carolinas helps African Americans and underprivileged communities by providing workforce development, education, housing support, health access, and advocacy programs that drive economic mobility and long‑term stability. It equips individuals with certifications, career pathways, and essential resources to achieve financial independence and social justice.

name: MYEP
quote: Our mission is to make sure every young person has the support and opportunities they need to succeed today and build a brighter future.
description: Provides paid work experiences to help high-school aged teens to develop career goals, stay in school, achieve academically, and develop social skills needed to succeed in work and life.

name: Charlotte Works
quote: Leading the development of a skilled and in-demand workforce by engaging businesses, aligning community partners, empowering and connecting job seekers to meaningful employment, and fostering inclusive economic growth.
description: Charlotte Works connects job seekers and employers, provides training, analyzes and shares data to educate the public on workforce needs, and aligns workforce initiatives to help the economic growth of Charlotte.

name: NC Works
quote: This website helps employers and job seekers. They all are united!
description: NCWorks in North Carolina’s statewide workforce system that connects job-seekers, employers, and training programs. It also helps residents find jobs, build skills, and access career services while supporting employers with hiring, training, and talent development.
address: 301 North Wilmington Street, Raleigh, North Carolina 27601

name: MeckWorks
quote: MeckWorks and NC Works connect job seekers to local businesses that are hiring right now.
description: MeckWorks helps Mecklenburg County residents facing significant life barriers gain rapid employment, stability, and long‑term economic mobility through integrated social‑work support, job placement, and wrap‑around services.

name: Trees Charlotte
quote: ...we must take a more aggressive approach to ensure the continued health and well-being of our tree canopy, and we must actively plant thousands of trees to account for growth and development in our city and around the region.
description: TreesCharlotte is a nonprofit that aims to partner with the community to plant and care for trees, and grow a healthier canopy for everyone. TreesCharlotte’s mission is to have a community with a shared responsibility for trees. You can volunteer or donate to support TreesCharlotte.
address: 4404 Stuart Andrew Blvd Suite M, Charlotte, NC 28217

name: Sustain Charlotte
quote: We work to address the region’s most pressing challenges
description: Sustain Charlotte is an organization that addresses the region’s most pressing challenges, which includes: carbon emissions, environmental conservation, sustainable transportation, and more. To support this organization, you can volunteer.

name: Clean AIRE NC
quote: We advocate for clean 'AIRE', Action and Innovation to Restore the Environment
description: CleanAIRE NC is an organization that wants everyone in North Carolina to have clean air and a healthy environment. They teach people about air pollution, push for better climate policies, and work with communities to create real solutions. Their goal is to make North Carolina a cleaner, safer place to live now and in the future.

name: Catawba Riverkeeper
quote: Catawba Riverkeeper strives for a community where everyone works in partnership to preserve and protect the vital waters of the Catawba
description: With a staff consisting of scientists, activists, and enthusiasts, the Catawba Riverkeeper organization advocates for the Catawba river, which is the river that flows through the Charlotte Metropolitan Area and provides water to 2 million residents.

name: Storm Water Services
quote: Charlotte-Mecklenburg Storm Water Services staff includes both City of Charlotte and Mecklenburg County employees. Staff coordinates closely to provide comprehensive stormwater services and ensure service to residents is seamless.
description: Charlotte‑Mecklenburg Storm Water Services is the team that takes care of storm drains, creeks, and flood‑prone areas in Charlotte. They fix drainage problems, monitor water quality, and run projects that help prevent flooding and pollution. Their goal is to keep neighborhoods safe and protect the environment by making sure rainwater flows where it’s supposed to and doesn’t harm local waterways.

name: Safe Alliance
quote: We provide hope and healing to those impacted by domestic violence, sexual assault, and human trafficking
description: Safe Alliance is an organization that helps people in the Charlotte area who are dealing with domestic violence or sexual assault. They offer a 24/7 crisis hotline, emergency shelter, counseling, legal support, and programs that help survivors stay safe and rebuild their lives. Their goal is to make sure survivors have the support they need to heal and move forward.

name: Benevolent Bridges
quote: Creating a sustainable impact, fostering hope and connection within the community.
description: Benevolent Bridges is an organization that provides assistance to the homeless through a comprehensive approach to restoring dignity, promoting health, and fostering a sense of community connection for those experiencing homelessness.

name: Crisis Assistance Ministry
quote: Help, Hope & Understanding, for people struggling with limited financial resources
description: The mission of Crisis Assistance Ministry is to provide assistance and advocacy for people in financial crisis, helping them move toward self-sufficiency. They offer emergency rent assistance, free essential items and long-term financial coaching.

name: Second Harvest Food Bank of Metrolina
quote: Together we CAN end hunger
description: The Second Harvest Food Bank of Metrolina provides quality food and grocery items to assist people in need in Charlotte and surrounding counties.

name: Habitat Charlotte Region
quote: Habitat Charlotte Region focuses on affordable homeownership
description: Habitat Charlotte Region’s Community Resources page connects local residents with programs that support housing stability, financial wellness, and basic needs. It’s designed to help individuals and families find trustworthy assistance beyond Habitat’s own services.

name: Hope Haven Inc
quote: The best way to predict your future is to create it.
description: Hope Haven is a Community that provides recovery for individuals and families impacted by substance use disorder, so they can build healthy independent lives.

name: Hearts & Hands Food Pantry
quote: Simply the kindest people I have ever met. So professionally ran. Especially during the Covid pandemic.
description: Hearts & Hands Food Pantry is a food bank that provides shelf stable and perishable groceries, personal care products, baby needs including diapers and formula, and pet supplies.

name: Families Forward Charlotte
quote: At Families Forward Charlotte, we aim to help local families take the necessary steps towards long-term financial stability.
description: Families Forward Charlotte works with local families who are struggling financially and helps them get on a path toward long-term stability. They match each family with a trained volunteer who supports them for one to two years, offering guidance, encouragement, and practical help.

name: Ballantyne Families Helping Neighbors in Need
quote: Because of your generous support, I was able to provide for my boys as a single mom.
description: Ballantyne Families provides food assistance to struggling families who attend or work in the Ballantyne area public schools through seasonal giving events and a food pantry.

name: Smart Start of Meck
quote: The Dolly Parton Imagination Library has really helped grow my 2-year-old son's love of reading! He gets so excited when a new book comes in the mail and loves picking it up to read on his own
description: Smart Start of Mecklenburg County helps young children in Charlotte get the best possible start in life by supporting families, childcare providers, and community programs that focus on early learning, health, and development. Like the Dolly Parton Imagination Library that helps kids read!

name: Thompson
quote: Our five-year plan is about more than numbers—it’s about transforming lives. By 2030, we will serve 8,000 children and families each year, ensuring every child has the opportunity to heal, grow, and thrive.
description: Thompson is an organization that is dedicated to transforming lives by focusing on Prevention, Mental Health, and Foster care. They work tirelessly to ensure all children are healthy, all families are thriving, and all communities are strong.
